requirements: Written consent form signed by knowledge holder before recording begins
Form explains: what will be recorded, how it will be stored, who may access it, permitted uses
Knowledge holder retains right to withdraw consent at any time before publication
Interviewer provides a copy of the signed form to the knowledge holder
Individual written consent from the knowledge holder (see standard protocol requirements)
Community-level consent from the relevant First Nations band council, tribal council, or designated governance body
Documentation that the community governance body has reviewed and approved the scope of documentation

Explicit statement of OCAP principles: Ownership — knowledge belongs to the nation; Control — nation determines how it is used; Access — nation may access and review all documentation; Possession — interviewer does not store nation data beyond agreed scope
Research data management plan reviewed by the community governance body before fieldwork begins
Community has the right to request destruction of recordings or transcriptions at any time
FNIGC OCAP® principles apply — see fnigc.ca for authoritative guidance
All requirements of the OCAP-compliant protocol
Research proposal reviewed and approved by the relevant Inuit organization (ITK, Nunavut Tunngavik Inc., Makivvik, Inuvialuit Regional Corporation, or Nunatsiavut Government as applicable)
ITK National Inuit Strategy on Research (NISR) compliance documentation prepared before fieldwork
Data sovereignty statement: all Inuit knowledge collected remains under Inuit governance
Inuktitut transcription reviewed by fluent Inuktitut speaker from the relevant dialect region (Nunavut, Nunavik, Inuvialuit, Labrador)
Research findings shared with the relevant Inuit organization before any public release
Any digital publication of Inuktitut content requires approval from the Inuit organization with jurisdiction over that dialect region
Consent obtained at the community level through the appropriate governance structure (varies by nation)
Individual knowledge holder's consent obtained as a secondary layer within the community consent framework
Community has pre-approved the scope and purpose of documentation through a community meeting or council resolution
Knowledge described as community property: no individual is positioned as the sole owner or authority
Attribution reflects community ownership: 'Shared by [Name], [Nation] community knowledge keeper' not 'Interview with [Name]'
Community review gate required before publication — community must approve how their collective knowledge is represented
